2605.14972.title: Viverra: Text-to-Code with Guarantees
2605.14972.paper_summary: A fundamental limitation of Text-to-Code is that no guarantee can be obtained about the correctness of the generated code. Therefore, to ensure its correctness, the generated code still has to be reviewed, tested, and maintained by developers. However, parsing through LLM-generated code can be tedious and time-consuming, potentially negating the productivity gains promised by AI-coding tools. To address this challenge, we present Viverra, a system that automatically produces formally verified annotations alongside generated code to aid user's understanding of the generated program. Given a natural-language task description, Viverra prompts an LLM to synthesize a C program together with candidate assertions expressing safety and correctness properties. It then verifies those assertions in a compositional and best-effort manner via a portfolio of bounded model checkers. Evaluation on 18 diverse programming tasks suggests that Viverra can efficiently generate code with verified assertions, and that these assertions improve users' performance on code-comprehension tasks in a user study with more than 400 participants.
2605.14972.paper_summary_zh: 一個 Text-to-Code 的基本限制是無法保證生成的代碼的正確性。因此，為了確保其正確性，生成的代碼仍然需要由開發者進行審查、測試和維護。然而，解析 LLM 生成的代碼可能是繁瑣且耗時的，這可能會抵消 AI 編碼工具所承諾的生產力提升。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了 Viverra，一個自動生成正式驗證註釋的系統，與生成的代碼一起幫助用戶理解生成的程序。給定一個自然語言任務描述，Viverra 促使 LLM 合成一個 C 程序，並生成表達安全性和正確性屬性的候選斷言。然後，它通過一組有界模型檢查器以組合和最佳努力的方式驗證這些斷言。對 18 個不同編程任務的評估表明，Viverra 可以有效生成帶有驗證斷言的代碼，並且這些斷言在一項超過 400 名參與者的用戶研究中提高了用戶在代碼理解任務上的表現。
2605.14972.author: Haoze Wu et.al.
2605.14972.authors: Haoze Wu, Rocky Klopfenstein, Keith Farkas, Nina Narodytska
